title: SunTech Medical Tango+ Vital Signs Monitor - Cuff Not Inflating During Treadmill Test
description: Addresses failure of the BP cuff to inflate during stress testing due to connection, cuff, hose, or system setup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop the stress test if the cuff is not functioning properly. Ensure the patient is stable and monitored by alternate means if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to initiate a manual BP cycle during the test or in standby mode.

steps.title: Check Cuff and Hose Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the cuff tubing from cuff to monitor. Ensure hose is fully seated at the Tango+ port. Check for loose, cracked, or disconnected fittings.

steps.title: Inspect the Cuff for Damage or Incorrect Size
steps.instructions: Check for tears, worn Velcro, or internal bladder leaks. Verify proper cuff size for patient. Swap with a known good cuff if available.

steps.title: Check Hose for Kinks or Obstructions
steps.instructions: Trace the full length of tubing. Look for sharp bends, pinching, or internal blockage. Ensure tubing is not caught under equipment or treadmill components.

steps.title: Verify Mode and Trigger Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm correct mode (exercise/stress mode) is selected. Verify trigger source (ECG gating or manual) is functioning.

steps.title: Check for Audible Pump Operation
steps.instructions: Initiate a BP cycle and listen for pump activity. Note any unusual noise or complete silence.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Replace cuff and hose, then retry inflation.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the Tango+ off, wait 10–15 seconds, power back on, and retry BP measurement.

CCR.Complaint: Cuff would not inflate during treadmill stress test on Tango+ monitor.
CCR.Cause: Failed internal pump—no audible operation during inflation attempt after ruling out cuff and hose.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent for repair; verified function with known good accessories prior to escalation.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or absence of alarms
Final device status (removed from service or returned to use)

title: SunTech Medical Tango+ Vital Signs Monitor - NIBP Reading Inaccurate Under Motion
description: Inaccurate or unstable blood pressure readings during patient movement, exercise stress testing, or motion-related conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on inaccurate readings for clinical decisions. Move the patient to a stable monitoring method if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Run a BP measurement while observing the patient’s activity level. Determine if readings fluctuate or fail only during motion.

steps.title: Check Cuff Placement and Fit
steps.instructions: Ensure proper cuff size, snug fit, and correct positioning on the upper arm. Reapply cuff if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Cuff Tubing and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify tubing is fully connected with no kinks, cracks, or leaks, and connections are secure.

steps.title: Confirm Use of Motion-Tolerant Mode (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Verify the device is set to stress/exercise mode and proper measurement method is selected.

steps.title: Check ECG Signal Integration (If Used)
steps.instructions: Ensure ECG leads are properly attached with a clean signal and synchronized with BP measurement.

steps.title: Minimize External Motion Interference
steps.instructions: Support the patient’s arm, reduce movement, and avoid external interference during measurement.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Cuff and Setup
steps.instructions: Swap cuff and tubing and repeat measurement under similar conditions.

steps.title: Perform Static BP Test
steps.instructions: Take a reading with the patient completely still to confirm device accuracy at rest.

CCR.Complaint: NIBP readings inaccurate during treadmill stress testing.
CCR.Cause: Improper cuff fit and arm movement during measurement.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced cuff, adjusted placement, and instructed proper arm positioning; verified accurate readings.

title: SunTech Medical Tango+ Vital Signs Monitor - Communication or PC Connection Failure
description: Troubleshooting Tango+ not communicating with stress test system due to cable, port, or configuration issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Stop the stress test and transition to manual BP monitoring or a backup device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm no BP readings appear on the PC, check for communication errors, and verify Tango+ is not transmitting data.

steps.title: Check Physical Communication Cable
steps.instructions: Inspect, reseat, and replace the communication cable if needed.

steps.title: Verify Correct Port Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure cable is connected to correct ports and adapters are secure.

steps.title: Check PC Software and COM Port Settings
steps.instructions: Verify correct COM port and confirm device appears in Device Manager.

steps.title: Restart Both Devices
steps.instructions: Power cycle the Tango+ and PC to clear communication issues.

steps.title: Check USB-to-Serial Adapter
steps.instructions: Verify drivers, swap adapter, and confirm COM port recognition.

steps.title: Verify Tango+ Communication Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure communication mode and protocol settings are correct.

steps.title: Test with Alternate PC or Port
steps.instructions: Use another PC or port to isolate device vs system issue.

CCR.Complaint: Tango+ not communicating with stress test system; no BP readings displayed on PC.
CCR.Cause: Failed USB-to-serial adapter preventing communication between monitor and PC.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced adapter and verified proper COM port communication with stress system.

title: SunTech Medical Tango+ Vital Signs Monitor - Self-Test or Calibration Failure
description: Troubleshooting Tango+ self-test or calibration failure due to setup issues, accessories, or external factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient testing. Do not perform calibration or troubleshooting during an active stress test.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Failure
steps.instructions: Power on the Tango+ and observe the exact message or behavior. Determine if the self-test fails at startup, calibration fails when initiated, or if any error codes or prompts are present.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Stability
steps.instructions: Ensure the unit is connected to a known good outlet or has sufficient battery charge. Verify the power cord is secure and check for intermittent power loss.

steps.title: Inspect and Reseat Cuff and Tubing
steps.instructions: Remove and reconnect all NIBP accessories. Check cuff tubing for cracks, leaks, or kinks. Ensure proper connection at the device port and confirm correct cuff size and type.

steps.title: Check for Environmental or Setup Issues
steps.instructions: Verify the device is on a stable, level surface with no excessive vibration and no active pressure in the system.

steps.title: Perform Manual Calibration Procedure
steps.instructions: Initiate calibration per the device interface, follow on-screen prompts, ensure the cuff is not applied to a patient, and allow full completion without interruption.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: Power cycle the Tango+ by turning it off completely, waiting 10–15 seconds, and powering it back on to repeat the self-test.

steps.title: Check for Repeated Failure Patterns
steps.instructions: Observe if the failure occurs at the same point each time, listen for unusual noises, and check for any error messages or codes.

CCR.Complaint: Tango+ failing self-test and unable to complete calibration prior to stress test.
CCR.Cause: Calibration failure persisted after verifying power, tubing integrity, and performing manual calibration—likely internal pressure sensing issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent to Clinical Engineering shop for further evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior during self-test or calibration

title: SunTech Medical Tango+ Stress BP Monitor - Power or Battery Errors
description: Power failures, unit not turning on, unexpected shutdowns, or battery-related issues affecting operation during stress testing.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the device is in use during a stress test, stop the test and transition to an alternate monitoring method.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on the unit and observe no power, intermittent shutdown, battery not charging, or error messages related to power.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Plug the device into a known working outlet, test the outlet with another device, and ensure hospital-grade outlet is functional.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Check the AC power cord for damage, loose connections, and proper seating. Reseat both ends securely.

steps.title: Observe Power Indicators
steps.instructions: With AC connected, check for power LED, charging indicator, and screen response.

steps.title: Attempt Battery Operation (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power and attempt to power on using battery to determine if battery is functional or failed.

steps.title: Check Battery Charging Behavior
steps.instructions: Reconnect AC power and observe charging indicators, battery level changes, and any alerts.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC, remove battery if accessible, wait 30 seconds, then reconnect and power on.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Environmental Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect for excessive heat, blocked vents, or improper environment conditions.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Power Source or Cord (If Available)
steps.instructions: Swap with a known good compatible cord or outlet to confirm external accessory failure.

CCR.Complaint: Tango+ monitor would not power on and failed during stress test setup.
CCR.Cause: Failed internal battery not accepting charge despite verified AC power.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for repair; battery replacement required.

helpfulDetails: Presence of heat or unusual smell
